page_content: ACME Human Resources Policy

Performance Reviews:
- Performance reviews are conducted annually in December.
- Mid-year reviews take place in June to track goals.

Remote Work:
- Remote work is permitted up to 2 days per week with manager approval.
- Core working hours are between 10:00 and 16:00.
HR Leave Policy

Overview:
Employees are entitled to 20 days of paid annual leave per calendar year. Leave is accrued pro-rata from
the employee's start date and is scheduled through the HR self-service portal.

Entitlement and Accrual:
- Full-time employees: 20 days per year, prorated for part-time staff.
- Public holidays are in addition to annual leave.
- New hires begin accruing leave from their first day; entitlement for the first year is calculated pro-rata.

Carryover and Expiry:
- Up to 5 unused days may be carried into the following year with manager approval.
- Any carried-over days must be used within the first 90 days of the new year or they will expire.

Types of Leave:
- Annual Leave: Planned time off for vacations and personal rest.
- Sick Leave: Employees are entitled to 10 days of paid sick leave per year; long-term sickness requires a
	medical certificate and coordination with HR.
- Emergency / Compassionate Leave: Short-term paid leave for bereavement or family emergencies; contact HR.
- Parental Leave: Governed by local legislation; contact HR for company-specific top-ups and eligibility.

Requesting Leave:
- Submit leave requests at least 2 weeks in advance for planned time off via the HR portal.
- For urgent or last-minute leave, notify your manager and HR as soon as possible.

Unpaid Leave:
- Unpaid leave may be granted at the discretion of the manager and HR; terms will be agreed in writing.

Contact:
For questions about leave balances, exceptions, or special cases, contact the HR team through the HR portal or
email hr@company.example.
Expense Policy

Purpose:
This policy describes what expenses are reimbursable, the approval workflow, and documentation requirements.

Submission and Timing:
- Expense reports should be submitted within 30 days of the expense date.
- Expenses submitted after 60 days require manager justification and may be declined.

Reimbursable Expenses:
- Travel (airfare, train, public transport) when pre-approved for business purposes.
- Accommodation for overnight business travel at reasonable rates.
- Meals while traveling for business or during approved client entertainment.
- Office supplies and small equipment purchases approved by your manager.

Non-Reimbursable Expenses:
- Personal expenses, family travel, fines (parking, traffic), and alcohol not associated with approved
	client entertainment.

Approval and Documentation:
- All expense items require original receipts or electronic equivalents.
- Expenses above $500 require manager and finance approval before reimbursement.
- Use the corporate expense system and attach receipts; company card transactions should be reconciled
	on the monthly statement.

Per Diem and Currency:
- Per-diem rules apply for multi-day travel; check the travel appendix for regional rates.
- For foreign currency expenses, submit receipts and the amount in local currency; finance will use the
	company exchange rate on the date of the transaction.

Audit and Record Retention:
- Finance may audit expense reports; retain receipts for 6 months after submission.

Contact:
Submit questions to finance@company.example or consult the Finance section on the intranet.
ACME Corporate Travel Policy

Booking and Approvals:
- Employees booking travel must use the corporate travel portal.
- All business travel must be approved by the department manager before booking.

Air Travel:
- Economy class must be used for all domestic flights and flights under 6 hours.
- Business class is permitted for international flights exceeding 6 hours with VP approval.

Meals and Accommodations:
- Hotel bookings are capped at $200 per night for standard business destinations.
- Daily meal expenses are reimbursed up to $75 per day.
- Alcoholic beverages are not reimbursable.
IT Security

Overview:
This document outlines key requirements and best practices to protect company systems and data.

Account Security:
- Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is required for all corporate accounts.
- Use a company-approved password manager and follow the password complexity rules.
- Never share credentials or one-time codes; legitimate IT staff will never ask for your password.

Device and Network:
- Keep devices up to date with the latest OS and security patches.
- Use disk encryption and ensure device screen locks are enabled with a password or biometric.
- Only connect to company resources over the corporate VPN when off-network.

Phishing and Suspicious Activity:
- Do not click links or open attachments from unknown senders.
- Report suspected phishing emails immediately to security@company.example and delete them.

Software and Data:
- Install only approved software from the company software catalog.
- Classify sensitive data as Confidential; do not store confidential data on personal devices.
- Use company-approved collaboration tools for sharing documents.

Incident Response:
- If you suspect an account compromise or data leak, contact the security team immediately and
	change affected passwords.

Contact:
Security team: security@company.example. For urgent incidents call the IT hotline listed on the intranet.
Public FAQ
Welcome to the company public FAQ. This page contains quick answers to common employee questions.

General:
- Office hours: Monday–Friday, 9:00–17:30 local time.
- Company holiday schedule is published on the intranet's calendar.

Getting Help:
- For IT support, submit a ticket via the IT service portal or email it-support@company.example.
- For HR inquiries (payroll, benefits, leave), check the HR portal or email hr@company.example.

Onboarding and Policies:
- New employees should review the onboarding checklist and complete mandatory security and compliance training
	during their first week.
- Company policies, employee handbook, and code of conduct are available on the intranet.

Where to Find Documents:
- Corporate templates, expense forms, and travel guides are in the Policies & Forms section of the intranet.

Other Questions:
If you can't find what you're looking for, contact the internal help desk or ask your manager for direction.
